Class AbstractCallStackBasedStopCondition

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.AbstractCallStackBasedStopCondition
All Implemented Interfaces:
StopCondition<Goal>
Direct Known Subclasses:
StepOverSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition, StepReturnSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition
public abstract class AbstractCallStackBasedStopCondition extends Object implements StopCondition<Goal>
Provides the basic functionality for StopConditions which stops the auto mode when the call stack size of the starting set node has a special difference to the call stack size of the current set node, e.g. "step over" or "step return".
See Also:
  • StepOverSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition
  • StepReturnSymbolicExecutionTreeNodesStopCondition

    getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)

  • Constructor Details

      public AbstractCallStackBasedStopCondition()

  • Method Details

    • getMaximalWork

      public int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout)
      Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar. Pass 0 to indicate unknown size.
      Specified by:
      getMaximalWork in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      Returns:
      The maximal amount of work or 0 if it is unknown.

    • isGoalAllowed

      public boolean isGoalAllowed(Goal goal, int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied)
      Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected ProofGoal chosen by the GoalChooser before it is applied. If it is not allowed the apply strategy will stop.
      Specified by:
      isGoalAllowed in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      goal - The current ProofGoal on which the next rule will be applied.
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the proof search (apply strategy) has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      Returns:
      true rule application is allowed, false rule application is not allowed so stop apply strategy

    • isCallStackSizeReached

      protected abstract boolean isCallStackSizeReached(int initialCallStackSize, int currentCallStackSize)
      Checks if the call stack size limit is reached.
      Parameters:
      initialCallStackSize - The call stack size of the initial set node.
      currentCallStackSize - The call stack size of the current set node.
      Returns:
      true limit reached, false limit node reached.

    • shouldStop

      public boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
      Specified by:
      shouldStop in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      true stop strategy, false continue strategy and apply next rule.

    • getStopMessage

      public @NonNull String getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, long startTime, int countApplied, @Nullable SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Returns a human-readable message which explains why the previous StopCondition.shouldStop(int,long,long,int,SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
      Specified by:
      getStopMessage in interface StopCondition<Goal>
      Parameters:
      maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply.
      timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled.
      startTime -

      The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via

      invalid reference 
      #currentTimeMillis()
      countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
      singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
      Returns:
      The human-readable message which explains the stop reason.
